market_summary: Massive risk-on day. Iran declared Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial traffic, triggering broad rally. S&P 500 hit fresh record at 7,126. Nasdaq Composite 13th straight gain (longest since 1992). Russell 2000 new ATH at 2,776.90. Oil cratered: WTI -11.5% to $83.78, Brent -10.8% to $88.67 — second-largest single-day drop since war began. Energy stocks (XOM, BP, CVX) sold off hard. Dollar weakened to 97.70 (DXY), third consecutive weekly decline. Bitcoin surged past $77K on risk-on momentum. Gold held near $4,878 despite risk-on, showing structural bid. Silver broke above $80. Fed rate at 3.50-3.75%, one cut still expected in 2026 but timing unclear due to war-driven inflation uncertainty.
key_events_tomorrow: April 18 (Saturday): Markets closed. No scheduled releases.
April 21 (Monday): US-Iran ceasefire expires — CRITICAL binary event. Either extended or escalation resumes.
April 21: Capital One Q1 earnings (after close), Halliburton Q1 earnings (morning)
April 22: Alphabet (GOOGL) Q1 earnings, Tesla (TSLA) Q1 earnings — major market movers
April 29: Amazon, Meta Platforms, Block earnings
OPEC+ next meeting: May 3 — review of 206K bpd April output hike
overnight_risks: US-Iran ceasefire expires April 21 — THE dominant risk. If not extended, oil spikes back toward $100+, risk-off across all assets. Weekend headlines critical.
Asia session Friday was cautious: Nikkei -1.75% to 58,476 (profit-taking after record high), CSI300 -0.17%, Hang Seng -1.01%. Suggests some skepticism about ceasefire durability.
Oil crash -11% today may trigger forced covering in energy longs and commodity funds — potential secondary effects on metals positioning.
Bitcoin rally past $77K may face resistance at $78K double-top — ceasefire euphoria fading per CoinDesk analysis.
Earnings week ahead (GOOGL, TSLA, MSFT, AMZN, META, AAPL) could dominate sentiment and overshadow geopolitics.
MSTR holding 780,897 BTC at avg cost $75,577 — currently underwater. Any BTC pullback to $73K creates significant NAV pressure.

instrument_news.BTC-USD: Surged past $77K on Hormuz reopening risk-on wave, +5% in 24h. But CoinDesk notes ceasefire boost 'fizzling out' as traders want real-world results. Whale accumulation massive: 270,000 BTC accumulated by 1000+ BTC wallets in past 30 days (largest since 2013). Exchange reserves at 7-year low (2.21M BTC, 5.88% of supply). BTC ETFs saw $325M outflows on April 13 as capital rotated to ETH ETFs. Double-top resistance near $76-78K. Institutional accumulation strong but retail fear persists.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: Outperforming BTC as ETF flows rotate in. Weekly ETH ETF inflows hit $187M (strongest of 2026, reversing 3 weeks of $308M outflows). Single-day $120M inflow on Apr 7 alongside Morgan Stanley S-1 filing for dedicated ETH trust. Cumulative ETH ETF inflows now $11.68B. BlackRock ETHA dominates with $6.5B AUM. On-chain activity jumped 41% week-over-week. ETH/BTC ratio recovering.
instrument_news.MSTR: Rallied from ~$119 to ~$166 in 7 days (+29%). Three BTC purchases in April: (1) 4,871 BTC for $330M at avg $67.7K on Apr 1-5, (2) 13,927 BTC for $1B at avg $71.9K on Apr 13, (3) 22,337 BTC (largest 2026 purchase) funded via STRC preferred shares. Total holdings now 780,897 BTC at avg cost $75,577. Trading at massive discount to analyst consensus target of $367.64 (180% implied upside). TD Cowen, Jefferies, BTIG all bullish. Pure leveraged BTC proxy — BTC rally directly lifts MSTR.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Held near $4,878 despite massive risk-on day — shows structural bid. Previous ATH was $5,594.82 (Jan 29, 2026). Gold is NOT selling off on peace optimism, suggesting central bank buying and ETF flows provide floor. GLD holdings rose to 920 tonnes (up from 840 at year-end). Central bank purchases on track for 1,200+ tonnes in 2026. JPM/Goldman forecast $4,000-$6,300 range. UBP reaffirms $6,000 target. COT: producer/commercial positions remain bullish.
instrument_news.XAG-USD: Broke above $80 for first time in weeks. 6th consecutive annual supply deficit projected (150-200M oz shortfall in 2026). Fresnillo (world's #1 primary silver producer) cut 2026 guidance 9% to 42-46.5 Moz. Mexico mining moratorium continues (25% of global supply). China classified silver as strategic material, tightening exports. Oil crash helps silver via lower mining costs, but structural deficit unchanged. Michael Oliver predicting $500/oz long-term. Near-term forecast: potential move to $89 by Apr 23.

metals_outlook: Gold structural bull intact — holding near $4,878 even on massive risk-on day (Hormuz open). Central bank buying 1,200+ tonnes pace in 2026. GLD holdings 920t, up 80t since year-end. COT still bullish (producer/commercial). Key: gold NOT selling off on peace news = very strong signal. Silver broke $80, supply deficit deepening (6th year). Fresnillo guidance cut -9%, Mexico moratorium, China strategic classification all tightening supply. If ceasefire fails Monday, both metals spike. If ceasefire holds, gold may consolidate $4,700-4,900 but silver's industrial demand (solar/EV/AI) provides independent bid.
oil_outlook: WTI crashed 11.5% to $83.78 on Hormuz reopening. Brent -10.8% to $88.67. Second-largest single-day drop since war began. But ceasefire expires April 21 — binary event. OPEC+ added 206K bpd in April (SA +62K, Russia +62K). Next OPEC+ meeting May 3. If ceasefire holds: WTI floor near $80-85. If ceasefire fails: rapid spike back to $95-105. Energy stocks (XOM, BP, CVX) sold off hard. EIA noted sharp Q1 crude price increases. Oil crash helps disinflation narrative, supports Fed cut case.
sentiment_shift: Fear & Greed Index recovered dramatically: from 8 (extreme fear, early April) to 56 (greed) by April 16. One of the fastest sentiment swings in crypto history. The Apr 17 reading of 21 cited in the system may be stale or from a different index variant. The shift from single-digit fear to greed reflects: (1) Hormuz reopening, (2) oil crash easing inflation fears, (3) BTC whale accumulation, (4) ETH ETF inflows rotating. However, ceasefire expiry on April 21 is a sentiment cliff — if it fails, expect rapid reversal back to extreme fear.
dxy_analysis: DXY fell to 97.70, down 0.52% on the day. Third consecutive weekly decline (-2.38% over past month). Dollar weakness driven by: (1) reduced safe-haven demand as Hormuz reopens, (2) oil crash easing inflation expectations, (3) lower probability of additional Fed tightening. Fed funds at 3.50-3.75%, one cut expected in 2026. Dollar weakness is tailwind for gold, silver, BTC, and all risk assets. Key level: if DXY breaks below 97, could accelerate to 95 — very bullish for metals and crypto.
swedish_market: Riksbank held rate at 1.75% for 4th consecutive meeting (March). Core inflation (CPIF ex-energy) dropped to 1.1% in March — lowest since July 2021. Swedish Spring Budget presented April 13 with SEK 7.7B in measures. VAT on food halved in April 2026 (temporarily lowers inflation). SEK was best-performing G10 currency in 2025 (+16.8% vs USD). Economy growing 2.5% in 2026. Avanza warrant trading hours: 08:15-21:55 CET. Oil crash positive for Swedish consumers and inflation outlook.

gold.note: COT data as of Apr 7 (latest available). Producer/commercial positions remain bullish with no signs of trend reversal despite elevated prices.
gold.trend: reducing longs slightly, adding shorts — but still heavily net long

silver.note: COT data as of Apr 7. Stable positioning.
silver.trend: slightly reducing longs and shorts — stable bullish positioning

critical_binary_events.if_positive: Oil stays $80-85, risk-on continues, DXY breaks 97, BTC targets $80K, gold consolidates $4,700-4,900
critical_binary_events.if_negative: Oil spikes to $100+, Hormuz re-blocked, risk-off across board, BTC back to $70K, gold spikes to $5,000+, F&G crashes back to extreme fear
